slides.title: Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 4: Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions. Welcome to Unit 4: Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions! In this unit you will explore how cities grow, how people move through them, and how communities around the world are finding creative solutions to make urban life better for everyone. You will read about transport systems, green innovations, and the challenges that cities face in East Africa and beyond. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a passage about city transport and discuss its ideas in English
Use prepositions of time and place (in, on, at) correctly and confidently
Form the passive voice in the past simple, present simple, and with modal verbs
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a passage about city transport and discuss its ideas in english. you will also use prepositions of time and place (in, on, at) correctly and confidently. you will also form the passive voice in the past simple, present simple, and with modal verbs. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: melancholy, exuberant, elated, vulnerable, wary, famished. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions: source text 1
Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions: source text 1 and Transport, Cities, and Urban Solutions: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Prepositions of Time and Place
bullets: What it means and why it matters Prepositions are small but powerful words that show relationships between other words in a sentence. The three most...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Prepositions of Time and Place. What it means and why it matters Prepositions are small but powerful words that show relationships between other words in a sentence. The three most important prepositions of time and place are in, on, and at. Using them correctly makes your English sound natural. Getting them wrong is one of the most common mistakes learners make, so this lesson is worth studying carefully. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
